title: Block same-side re-entry on a slug after gap_stop exit for the rest of the session
status: QUEUED — run after SESSION_MARKET_TRADE_CAP_001 is APPROVED (same file)
depends_on: SESSION_MARKET_TRADE_CAP_001 (APPROVED) — both tasks touch bot_core.py, must not run concurrently

context.problem: After a gap_stop exit on mlb-foo BUY_YES, the bot re-enters on the same slug/side within the current 60-min cooldown window or immediately after it expires. Tonight: mlb-col-sd had a gap_stop then was re-entered. mlb-cws-kc had multiple gap_stops across the session on BUY_YES. mlb-hou-sea had gap_stops and was re-entered. A gap_stop means the market moved 30-90% against the position in a single interval — this is not a normal SL event, it is a market telling you the outcome is nearly decided.
evidence: mlb-cws-kc: gap_stops at trades 238, 246, 247, 251, 252 — all BUY_YES — bot kept re-entering BUY_YES between stops
mlb-hou-sea: gap_stops at 263, 265, 285 — re-entered BUY_YES between each
mlb-bos-stl: gap_stops at 278, 279 — entries at 0.12 then 0.03 (catastrophic falloff)
Total gap_stop damage tonight: -$499.99 across 21 trades
context.design: In bot_core.py, maintain a session-level set `_session_gap_stop_bans: set[tuple[str, str]]` tracking (slug, side) pairs that have had a gap_stop exit. At entry gate check, if (slug, side) is in this set, reject with 'post_gap_stop_session_ban'. This is separate from and stricter than the existing 60-min market cooldown.

objective: After any gap_stop exit on (slug, side), block new entries on that exact (slug, side) combination for the rest of the session. Does not block the opposite side.
forbidden_actions: Modify the existing 60-min market cooldown logic
required_work: 1. Add a module-level set in bot_core.py: `_session_gap_stop_bans: set = set()` (tuples of (slug, side)).
2. In the exit/close handling path where gap_stop is detected, add: `_session_gap_stop_bans.add((slug, side))`.
3. In the entry gate check (before or as part of check_entry_gates()), check if (slug, side) in _session_gap_stop_bans. If yes, reject with reason 'post_gap_stop_session_ban:{slug}:{side}'.
4. Log the rejection: 'BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates] slug=... reasons=[post_gap_stop_session_ban:...]'.
5. The ban is in-memory only (session-scoped). It resets on restart — this is intentional.
acceptance_criteria: A gap_stop on (slug, BUY_YES) blocks future BUY_YES entries on that slug for the session
A gap_stop on (slug, BUY_YES) does NOT block BUY_NO entries on that slug
The ban is logged as a check_entry_gates rejection with the correct reason string
The ban is in-memory (resets on restart) — no DB or state.json changes required
verification_required: Show the exact location in bot_core.py where the ban set is populated (exit/close path)
Show the exact location where the ban is checked (entry gate path)
